hypothesis: If lattice design-property data are arranged as a tensor over design parameters, then tensor-completion-based surrogate models (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) will achieve higher R² in predicting Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus than baseline ML methods (Gaussian Process, XGBoost, MLP), particularly under limited or biased training sampling, because tensor completion exploits the low-rank multi-way structure and generalizes better across unobserved parameter combinations.
